mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer spending momentum; higher spending directly lifts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeding 25 basis points signals a shift to monetary tightening; higher risk-free rates raise equity discount rates and compress the present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of episodes where FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25 percentage points within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates coordinated strength across consumer spending and residential investment; this joint demand signal is not captured by either series alone and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST adds no marginal explanatory power (t-statistic less than 2.0) for 20-day SPX returns beyond the additive main effects in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a 3-month rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades; this produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2 percent, triggered UNRATE rises are not followed by significantly negative 5-day SPX returns (t-test p-value greater than 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization; sustained increases in industrial activity translate into higher corporate operating leverage and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 3-month changes in INDPRO show a Rank IC below 0.02 with 60-day SPX forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10 percent signals a contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness; this reduction in a key cyclical component of GDP produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when HOUST declines more than 10 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A negative monthly change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates net job losses and a deterioration in household income formation; this sudden weakening of the labor market triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative monthly changes in PAYEMS do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) mark the onset of an industrial recession; this persistent supply contraction signals falling corporate revenues and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Three consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of historical episodes.
